office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Incumbent Adison Richards faces school-board member David Olson and teacher Natalie Bornfleth in a race contrasting broad public-service priorities, fiscal accountability, and progressive revenue.

candidates.summary: Richards is an incumbent representative and nonprofit attorney emphasizing affordability, housing, transportation, education, health care, and reproductive rights.
highlights: Supports housing, ferries, education, and health care [S2]

experience.basis: Richards is an incumbent representative with an official legislative profile.

candidates.summary: Olson is a long-serving school-board member emphasizing school performance, parental involvement, public safety, controlled growth, and fiscal accountability.
highlights: Emphasizes school performance and parental involvement [S2]
Supports fiscal accountability and controlled growth [S2]

experience.basis: He brings long elected school-board service but no legislative tenure.

social.basis: He explicitly emphasizes parental involvement in schools.

candidates.summary: Bornfleth is a teacher, union representative, former shipyard worker, and Air Force veteran advocating progressive revenue and public investment.
highlights: Supports progressive revenue and education funding [S2]
Supports housing, transportation, and worker protections [S2]

spending.basis: She supports education funding and expanded transportation.

experience.basis: She has labor, education, military, and trades experience but no documented elected record.
